description:
Goal: finish the type-world arc by simplifying the remaining semantic bookkeeping that still assumes partial facts or lossy dependency digests. Signal: semantic closure tracks exact dependency snapshots, callsite facts are modeled as whole facts rather than split partial ones, and the docs ticket is reduced to writing down a settled engine instead of describing moving targets.

Strategy: after the local type world and exact fact revisions are in place, remove the leftover lossy closure digesting and duplicated callsite fact surfaces, then hand a quieter, more honest engine back to the docs/backend boundary.

Change:
- Replace `combined_fingerprint` and its XOR-based dependency digest with exact deduped dependency snapshots over fact keys and revisions.
- Rework semantic-closure settling to use those exact snapshots.
- Collapse duplicated partial callsite facts into a whole callsite-summary fact surface where downstream consumers wait on and read one logical fact.
- Update Compiler2 traces/tests to assert the quieter fact surface and dependency behavior.
- Refresh the docs ticket description if needed so it reflects the settled post-arc surface.

Why:
- Interned local type ids make exact key/value tracking cheap enough that lossy digests no longer buy us anything.
- One logical fact should be one fact key.
- Finishing this cleanup before docs keeps the docs ticket small and factual.

Checks:
- Semantic-closure tests prove unchanged dependencies do not retrigger closure/materialization and changed dependencies do.
- Callsite consumers wait on and read one logical fact surface.
- Quicksort/Enum.reduce trace assertions get quieter or stay equally strong with less bookkeeping.
title: Compiler2 closure and callsite facts: replace lossy dependency digests with exact snapshots
